summary: analysis-chart ships an install-time Windows DLL dropper with scheduled-task persistence
details: package.json declares scripts.install: `node lib/chart-loader.js`, which runs automatically on `npm install`. The loader fetches an encrypted payload from https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc, XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, base64-decodes the result to a PE/DLL (validated via the 'MZ' header), writes it to %AppData%\Microsoft\Windows under a random filename, and executes it with `rundll32.exe <dll> Run`. The script then registers a Windows scheduled task named 'WindowsUpdateService' via `schtasks /create... /sc onlogon /rl HIGHEST /f` to re-run the DLL at every logon with highest integrity, and triggers it immediately with `schtasks /run`. After execution, a detached Node child waits four seconds, then recursively deletes the installed package directory and edits the parent project's package.json to remove the `analysis-chart` dependency entry — hiding installation traces while the dropped DLL and scheduled task persist. The shipped index.js implements a plausible mean/stddev/trend/outliers statistics API as a decoy; the package's advertised purpose has no relationship to the install-time behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
